seo.title: Multimodal AI (Vision, Audio) implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping Multimodal AI (Vision, Audio) projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist outlines the technical requirements for moving multimodal AI features from prototype to production, focusing on input optimization, cost control, and reliability.

items.title: Resolution Downscaling
items.description: Resize images to the model's maximum effective resolution (e.g., 2048px for GPT-4o) before transmission to reduce latency and token costs.

items.title: Audio Chunking and Formatting
items.description: Split audio files into segments under 25MB and convert to optimized formats like MP3 or OGG to meet API payload limits.

items.title: Visual Token Estimation
items.description: Implement logic to calculate visual tokens based on image dimensions and detail mode (high vs. low) to predict costs before API calls.

items.title: EXIF Metadata Stripping
items.description: Remove non-essential metadata from image files to reduce payload size and prevent leaking sensitive location or device data.

items.title: Aspect Ratio Normalization
items.description: Verify that images are padded or cropped to aspect ratios supported by the model to prevent distortion in visual understanding.

items.title: Local Rate Limiting
items.description: Implement a token-bucket or leaky-bucket algorithm to match your provider's Tier-specific RPM (Requests Per Minute) and TPM (Tokens Per Minute).

items.title: Media Content Hashing
items.description: Generate SHA-256 hashes for input media and cache model responses to avoid paying for redundant processing of identical files.

items.title: Model Detail Mode Selection
items.description: Default to 'low' detail mode for vision tasks not requiring OCR or fine-grained texture analysis to save 50-80% on token costs.

items.title: Tiered Model Fallbacks
items.description: Route simple visual classification tasks to cheaper models (e.g., Gemini Flash) while reserving GPT-4o for complex reasoning.

items.title: Usage Quota Alerts
items.description: Set hard spend limits and 50/75/90% threshold alerts at the API provider level to prevent unexpected billing spikes.

items.title: Async Processing for Long Audio
items.description: Use webhook-based architectures for audio files longer than 1 minute to avoid client-side timeouts during transcription.

items.title: Coordinate System Validation
items.description: Verify that bounding box outputs are correctly mapped to original image dimensions, accounting for any pre-processing scaling.

items.title: Exponential Backoff for 429s
items.description: Implement a retry strategy with jitter specifically for rate-limit and overloaded-model errors from multimodal endpoints.

items.title: Fallback for Corrupt Media
items.description: Implement validation steps to catch partial uploads or corrupted headers before sending payloads to the AI model.

items.title: Structured Output Enforcement
items.description: Use JSON mode or function calling to ensure vision model outputs (like OCR results) adhere to a parseable schema.

items.title: Short-lived Media URLs
items.description: Use pre-signed URLs with an expiration under 10 minutes when passing cloud storage objects to vision APIs.

items.title: Pre-inference Content Moderation
items.description: Run images through a specialized moderation API to block prohibited content before it reaches the expensive multimodal model.

items.title: PII Detection in Media
items.description: Scan images for faces or identity documents and apply blurring if your use case does not require processing personal identifiers.

items.title: Provider Data Retention Check
items.description: Confirm that the API provider's terms of service exclude your media inputs from being used for base model training.

items.title: Input Sanitization
items.description: Validate MIME types and magic numbers of uploaded files to prevent malicious file execution on your processing workers.

items.title: Vision Ground Truth Dataset
items.description: Maintain a set of 100+ 'gold standard' image-text pairs to test for regression when updating prompts or model versions.

items.title: Word Error Rate (WER) Baseline
items.description: Measure and log the WER for audio transcriptions against manual transcripts to detect accuracy drift in specific acoustic environments.

items.title: Time-To-First-Token (TTFT) Tracking
items.description: Monitor the latency of the initial response chunk for multimodal prompts to ensure the UI remains responsive.

items.title: Hallucination Verification
items.description: Implement a secondary LLM check to verify that visual descriptions match the labels extracted by a traditional CV model.

items.title: Prompt Versioning
items.description: Store the exact system prompt and model version used for every multimodal inference to enable debugging of visual logic errors.
